drafts.title: Goal state is event-sourced on the current session branch
drafts.compiledTruth: Goal state uses pi-goal-event entries as the only truth, while .pi-astack/goal/<sessionId>.json is only a materialized injection cache. Reconcile must replay getBranch root-to-leaf, never the whole tree. If the current branch has goal events, overwrite the view from them; if it has none, delete the view so switching to a pre-goal branch cannot keep injecting an old goal.

drafts.title: Active goals are injected each turn as sanitized tail context
drafts.compiledTruth: An active goal is appended to the system prompt tail every turn inside explicit markers, which counters compaction drift without disturbing prompt-cache prefixes. Non-active goals must have stale blocks stripped. Sub-agents do not receive the injected goal block, so a dispatch task brief is not shadowed. Injected text is sanitized for marker collisions, control and bidi characters, and capped in length.

drafts.title: Auto-continue spends bounded budget before sending continuation
drafts.compiledTruth: Auto-continue checks wall-clock and continuation-count budgets before any judge LLM spend. A continuation decrements budget by writing the event source first and persisting the view; only after both succeed may it send the follow-up. If either write fails, it does not send. This prevents restart or reconcile from rolling back the counter and re-spending the same budget.

drafts.title: The goal judge can classify progress but cannot change topology
drafts.compiledTruth: The goal judge has a closed output space of achieved, blocked, or continue. Parse or transport failure fails closed with no send and no state change. The judge cannot spawn agents, change topology, or veto structure; next_step is only free text for the next continuation message. Repeated uncertainty is biased toward visible budget spend or blocked rather than silent achieved.

drafts.title: Machine-authored user-role turns require deterministic provenance isolation
drafts.compiledTruth: A machine-authored user-role continuation must carry the shared [pi-goal-continuation goal_id=...] prefix, be classified by sediment as assistant provenance, and be skipped by directive recall. The window builder must preserve the head of user turns so the prefix survives truncation. Any future path that injects machine text as user role needs its own equivalent isolation design and ADR review; the goal prefix does not automatically cover new paths.

drafts.title: Workflow v1 keeps mutation behind three explicit gates
drafts.compiledTruth: Workflow v1 is an experimental channel with workflow.enabled default off and workflow.readOnly default true. A mutating stage requires all of: per-stage mutating:true in the DSL, workflow.readOnly=false in settings, and PI_MULTI_AGENT_ALLOW_MUTATING=1. Mutating work must happen inside dispatched sub-agents, not the main session. Weakening any gate or flipping defaults is productionization, not a small config tweak.
drafts.sourceHeading: ## 5. 两级通道与晋升机检定义

drafts.title: Workflow avoids H5 only while topology is a fixed declared DAG with no spawn tools
drafts.compiledTruth: A workflow stays outside hub dynamic dispatch only when its declared graph is fixed before execution, topology authority is outside the runtime LLM, and the engine has zero freedom to add, delete, or rewrite stages or edges. Declared failure policy may deterministically skip downstream work, and upstream LLM output may affect downstream content, but neither may alter the stage set or dependencies. Stage tools must reject dispatch_agent, dispatch_parallel, and any spawn-class tool; allowing them makes a stage a de facto hub and falls under the H5 gate.

drafts.title: Workflow DSL is constrained JSON data, not YAML or executable code
drafts.compiledTruth: The workflow DSL is JSON validated by schema and deterministic dry-run checks. YAML is rejected because agent-generated indentation is fragile; sandboxed JavaScript is rejected because orchestration skeletons must be structured data, not arbitrary code. Version 1 has strict boundaries: schema_version is 1, stage ids are globally unique, needs target only top-level stages, parallel children are agent-only and non-nestable, unknown or dispatch tools are rejected, and readOnly mode fails rather than silently stripping mutating tools.

drafts.title: Workflow failure routing must not be chosen by an LLM
drafts.compiledTruth: Stage terminal classification that drives on_fail must come only from deterministic signals such as dispatch terminal state, abort code, output path existence, and schema validation. A stage agent self-report or judge-style LLM choice of retry, degrade, abort, or downstream path is forbidden because it becomes runtime execution-subgraph selection. Degraded stages must still emit an output path with partial result or failure explanation, and the final workflow result must surface degraded stages.

drafts.title: Workflow traces do not become Tier-1 rules without human-originated signal
drafts.compiledTruth: Workflow definitions are saved only by explicit user action into the abrain workflows zone. Engine, judge, and sediment must not auto-save workflows. Execution traces are assistant-observed artifacts and are not Tier-1 injection material by default; auto-sedimenting traces into reusable workflows is postponed because an autonomous system must not feed its own outputs back as its own rules without a human-originated signal.
